command: env -u AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID -u AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY -u AWS_SESSION_TOKEN raws sts get-caller-identity --region us-east-1 2>&1
aws_command: env -u AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID -u AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY -u AWS_SESSION_TOKEN aws sts get-caller-identity --region us-east-1 2>&1
difference: raws says 'Failed to resolve AWS credentials: No credentials found. Configure credentials via environment variables ...' (exit 255); aws says 'aws: [ERROR]: An error occurred (NoCredentials): Unable to locate credentials. You can configure credentials by running "aws login".' (exit 253). Different error message format and exit code.

raws outputs 'The config profile (nonexistent-profile-xyz) could not be found' but aws outputs 'aws: [ERROR]: The config profile (nonexistent-profile-xyz) could not be found'. raws is missing the 'aws: [ERROR]: ' prefix. Exit codes both 255.
